page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p-redis.2xlarge is a KVM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.re6p server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 32.0 GB of system memory, which translates to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include any local storage or GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7025.92 and a PassMark CPU score of 8313.72. However, single-core capabilities are weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1318.98 and a weak single-threaded gzip compression rate of 23.01 GB/sec. Memory performance is average, with read-only large block bandwidth at 50.58 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 38.0 GB/sec. The instance achieves an average of 6.81 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 6.64 GB/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute performance, though the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a standard general-purpose hardware profile. The lack of local storage requires users to factor in the latency and cost of external block storage. Given the average memory bandwidth and dedicated core configuration, this instance is configured for workloads such as in-memory caching, web serving, and general-purpose database operations that do not require high-speed local disk I/O or intensive single-threaded processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6p-redis.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Baseline network performance is 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth capabilities, though single-core performance is weak. The instance achieves 6.81 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 6.64 GB/sec. Given its dedicated compute resources and average memory throughput, it is suited for in-memory caching, web serving, and database workloads that rely on network-attached storage.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual server with balanced memory and compute resources, optimized for network-attached workloads and in-memory caching.
